researched_category.notes: Squarespace is primarily a website building and hosting platform with e-commerce capabilities. The developer portal describes it as enabling developers to create fully custom sites and integrate with 3rd party tools.

one_liner.value: Website building and e-commerce platform that enables users to create and manage websites, online stores, and domains, with Commerce APIs for managing orders, products, inventory, and transactions.

methods.notes: Both API key and OAuth 2.0 are supported. API keys are used for custom/single-site applications (generated in site settings). OAuth 2.0 is used for commercial third-party extensions (requires registration form and review). The developer portal states: 'Depending on your development needs, you can use a generated API key or start the OAuth process with Squarespace to become a registered OAuth client.'

access.notes: API keys can be self-generated by any Squarespace site owner with a paid plan (at minimum Core/Plus plan for some APIs, Advanced plan for commerce APIs like Orders, Inventory, Transactions, Products). The support page states: 'Orders, Inventory, and Transactions are only available in the Commerce Advanced plan.' The developer portal states: 'With Squarespace's Commerce Advanced plan, you can develop a custom application for a Squarespace merchant site.' No free tier provides API access. OAuth client registration requires submitting a form and getting reviewed, but that's for building commercial extensions, not for using the API itself.

summary.value: Squarespace offers a comprehensive set of REST Commerce APIs covering Orders, Products, Inventory, Transactions, Profiles/Contacts, Analytics, Websites, and Webhook Subscriptions. All APIs are built on REST principles with HTTPS. Rate limit is 300 requests per minute. The developer portal documents endpoints, authentication, schemas, and provides detailed guidance.
summary.notes: The Commerce APIs include: Contacts API, Analytics API, Inventory API, Orders API, Products API, Profiles API, Transactions API, Webhook Subscriptions API. Also has a Websites endpoint for basic site info.

agent_interface.notes: Multiple community MCP servers exist: a GitHub project (BusyBee3333/squarespace-mcp-2026-complete) with 67 tools, Aerostack MCP, MCP Bundles, and viaSocket MCP. Composio has a toolkit stub listed but with 0 tools currently.

rationale.value: The Squarespace Commerce API is well-documented and feature-rich (broad REST API with OAuth2 support), and community MCP servers already exist demonstrating buildability. However, the API requires a paid Squarespace plan — specifically the Commerce Advanced (now 'Advanced') plan ($49+/month) to use Orders, Inventory, Products, and Transactions APIs. There is no free tier or sandbox for development without paying for a plan. Additionally, OAuth credentials for commercial/third-party use require submitting a form and awaiting approval. For an agent toolkit targeting a single merchant's own site, API keys can be self-generated from within a paid site's settings — this is self-serve but paywalled.
rationale.notes: Main blocker is the paid plan requirement (Commerce Advanced / Advanced tier). The API itself is well-documented and functional. A developer can build an agent toolkit today if they or their target user has a qualifying paid Squarespace plan.

s1.quote: The Squarespace Developer Platform enables developers to create fully custom sites and integrate with 3rd party tools.
s1.title: Home — Squarespace Developers

s3.quote: With Squarespace's Commerce Advanced plan, you can develop a custom application for a Squarespace merchant site. ... Depending on your development needs, you can use a generated API key or start the OAuth process with Squarespace to become a registered OAuth client.
s3.title: Authentication and permissions

s4.quote: Squarespace uses OAuth 2.0 to authorize third-party applications to integrate with Squarespace and consume Squarespace APIs.
s4.title: Accessing Squarespace APIs using OAuth 2.0

s5.quote: With Squarespace Commerce APIs, you can build applications to manage the commerce features of a Squarespace merchant site. Contacts API, Analytics API, Inventory API, Orders API, Products API, Profiles API, Transactions API, Webhook Subscriptions API.
s5.title: Overview of Commerce APIs

s6.quote: Forms are available in the Business plan or higher. Orders, Inventory, and Transactions are only available in the Commerce Advanced plan.
s6.title: Squarespace API keys

s7.quote: No, access to the Squarespace API is not available on free or entry-level tiers. You must be on the Core plan or above to generate a Squarespace API key, and certain permissions require the Commerce Advanced plan.
s7.title: How to Get Squarespace API Key: A Step-by-Step Guide

s8.quote: Squarespace Commerce APIs employ a rate limit of 300 requests per minute, an equivalent bandwidth of five requests per second.
s8.title: Rate limits

s9.quote: Squarespace APIs (Inventory API, Orders API, Products API, etc.) are built HTTPS. Squarespace APIs use REST principles in their design.
s9.title: Squarespace API - API2Cart

s10.quote: A comprehensive Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for Squarespace, providing complete integration with the Squarespace platform for website management, e-commerce operations, content creation, and analytics.
s10.title: BusyBee3333/squarespace-mcp-2026-complete

s11.quote: Squarespace Commerce API for managing orders, products, inventory, and transactions. Auth: API_KEY, OAUTH2. Composio Managed App Available? No. Tools: 0.
s11.title: Squarespace - Composio Toolkit | Composio

s12.quote: The Squarespace MCP gives Claude 12 tools covering inventories, orders, products.
s12.title: Squarespace MCP — Inventories, orders & products | Aerostack

limitations: The Squarespace pricing page is JavaScript-rendered and the fetched content did not include full plan pricing details. Plan names have changed over time (Personal→Basic, Business→Core, Commerce Basic→Plus, Commerce Advanced→Advanced). The exact pricing and plan naming were verified through secondary sources and the support/developer docs which reference 'Commerce Advanced plan' as the required tier for commerce APIs.
The 'Composio Managed App' for Squarespace has 0 tools listed, suggesting it's a stub/unimplemented.
Could not verify if there is an official Squarespace MCP server; only community ones were found.
